# Intake and Positioning

Use this reference when creating `01-client-brief.md` and `03-account-strategy.md`.

## Required Intake Fields

Collect every field below before moving into competitor research:

| Field | What to capture | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| Client name | Brand or operator name | Required for workspace naming |
| Industry | One clear vertical | Prevents mixed-topic planning |
| Offer | Product, service, or lead magnet | Determines monetization path |
| Target user | Age, gender, intent, pain point | Shapes note angle and language |
| Monetization path | WeChat lead, in-app sale, store visit, consultation, ad deal | Affects CTA timing |
| Proof assets | Photos, before/after, case studies, testimonials, founder story | Determines content realism |
| Operating capacity | Posts per day, editing ability, can they appear on camera | Sets feasible cadence |
| Existing account state | New account, old inactive account, active but weak account | Changes launch sequence |

Do not infer missing fields if the answer changes the plan materially.

## Positioning Rules

- Pick one main vertical per account.
- Limit subtopics to 3-5 recurring buckets.
- Prefer a niche users already search for over a broad aspirational identity.
- Name the account so a user can understand the vertical in one glance.
- Write the bio for search clarity first, personality second.

## Persona Selection

Choose one persona model and use it consistently:

| Persona | Use when | Strength | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| Expert mentor | The operator has domain credibility or results | Trust and education | Can sound stiff |
| Founder/operator | There is a real product and behind-the-scenes story | Commercial credibility | Becomes too brand-heavy easily |
| Diary-style practitioner | The operator is relatable and can share progress | Strong emotional pull | Needs disciplined topic boundaries |
| Curated brand voice | Team-run or multi-editor account | Scales across operators | Can feel generic without proof |

## Niche Selection Heuristics

Prefer a niche that scores well on all four axes:

1. Search demand: users clearly search the topic on-platform.
2. Proof availability: the client can show real examples, photos, or outcomes.
3. Repeatability: the team can produce 10+ useful notes fast.
4. Commercial fit: the note topics can eventually bridge to the offer.

Reject niches that require constant hot takes, depend on scraped content, or force the team into unsupported claims.

## Completion Standard

`01-client-brief.md` is complete only when:

- the client, industry, offer, audience, and monetization path are explicit
- the account starting state is known
- the proof assets and operating capacity are known
- the main vertical and subtopics are defined
